title: Explicit overflow operators +% +| — defend over method chain style
description: Ferrum uses operator syntax for wrapping (a +% b) and saturating (a +| b) arithmetic, rather than Rust's .wrapping_add(b) / .saturating_add(b) method calls. A reviewer may suggest 'just use method calls like Rust'. Defend: operator syntax is essential for readability in DSP, cryptography, and protocol code where wrapping arithmetic is the norm. Having to write (a.wrapping_add(b)).wrapping_add(c) instead of a +% b +% c is illegible. The operator approach is correct for a systems language targeting these domains.
